title: Saved stories never appear in the 'Saved' feed (saved-feed API returns empty despite save succeeding)
description: After clicking 'Save story' on a story card — which fills the bookmark icon and is confirmed by the server returning the story with "saved": true — switching the feed selector to the 'Saved' view shows 'No stories found.'. The just-saved story never appears in the Saved feed, so the save-and-view-saved journey cannot be completed.
reproduction_steps:
1. Open https://deploy-preview-8--blamy-broken-linear-digg-clone.netlify.app
2. On the top story card ('If you are asking for human attention, demonstrate human effort'), click the 'Save story' (bookmark) button. The bookmark icon fills, indicating the story is saved.
3. Click the feed selector (showing 'All Digg') and choose 'Saved'.
4. Observe the Saved view: it displays 'No stories found.' instead of the saved story.
expected_behavior: After saving a story, selecting the 'Saved' view should list that story so the user can find their bookmarked stories.
actual_behavior: The 'Saved' view shows 'No stories found.' even though the story was successfully saved (filled bookmark; server PATCH /api/stories/hn-48497609/save returned the story with "saved": true). The saved-feed request GET /api/feed?feed=saved returns {"feed":"saved","source":"hn","stories":[]}, an empty list.

chain.text: PATCH /api/stories/hn-48497609/save returned 200 with the story object's "saved" field set to true, so the application treats the story as saved (the bookmark icon is filled on the card).

root_cause.text: The server's saved-feed endpoint returns no stories even though the save endpoint reported the same story as saved. PATCH /api/stories/hn-48497609/save responded 200 with the story body containing "saved": true, but the subsequent GET /api/feed?feed=saved responded 200 with {"feed":"saved","source":"hn","stories":[]} — an empty list. These two server responses directly contradict each other for the same story: the server acknowledges the story is saved yet excludes it from the saved feed. This is a server-side defect — the saved feed does not include the story the server confirmed saving.

analysis.buggy_behavior: Saving a story succeeds (the bookmark icon fills and the server returns the story with saved:true), but the 'Saved' feed view shows 'No stories found.' — the saved story never appears in the Saved feed.

judge_assessment.assessment:
Evidence: Screenshot 1 shows the Digg feed with the first story's bookmark icon appearing filled/solid after clicking save. Screenshot 2 shows the feed selector changed to 'Saved' with 'No stories found.' displayed. Screenshot 3 confirms the same empty saved feed state. The evidence strongly supports the root cause: the PATCH save endpoint returns the story with saved:true (200), but the subsequent GET /api/feed?feed=saved returns an empty stories array (200), a clear server-side contradiction. All factual claims (saved:true in response, empty stories array, story-list.tsx rendering, story-card.tsx line references) are verified in the evidence results. The root cause is stated definitively as a server-side defect evidenced by contradictory API responses, without speculating about internal implementation. Interaction evidence requirements are met for both interaction steps.

Requirements: The bug report documents a clear, reproducible functional failure: saving a story succeeds (PATCH returns saved:true, bookmark icon fills), but switching to the Saved feed shows 'No stories found.' because GET /api/feed?feed=saved returns an empty stories array. The screenshots clearly show: [Image 1] the feed with the bookmark icon on the story card, [Image 2] the feed selector changed to 'Saved' showing the empty state, and [Image 3] confirming the persistent empty state. The root cause correctly identifies this as a server-side contradiction — the save endpoint confirms the story is saved but the saved feed endpoint returns no stories. This follows the guideline exception for server responses that contradict observable application state, so no backend recording analysis is required. The causal chain is well-traced with network evidence showing both the PATCH response (saved:true) and the GET response (empty stories array). Cross-run interference is not a plausible explanation since the save was performed by this journey and the server's own feed endpoint fails to return it. This is a clear core functionality failure that prevents the save-and-view-saved workflow.

PATCH /api/stories/hn-48497609/save responded 200 with the story body containing "saved": true, but the subsequent GET /api/feed?feed=saved responded 200 with {"feed":"saved","source":"hn","stories":[]} — an empty list. These two server responses directly contradict each other for the same story: the server acknowledges the story is saved yet excludes it from the saved feed. This is a server-side defect — the saved feed does not include the story the server confirmed saving.

root_cause.text: The server's saved-feed endpoint returns no stories even though the save endpoint reported the same story as saved. PATCH /api/stories/hn-48497609/save responded 200 with the story body containing "saved": true, but the subsequent GET /api/feed?feed=saved responded 200 with {"feed":"saved","source":"hn","stories":[]} — an empty list. These two server responses directly contradict each other for the same story: the server acknowledges the story is saved yet excludes it from the saved feed. This is a server-side defect — the saved feed does not include the story the server confirmed saving.

analysis.buggy_behavior: Saving a story succeeds (the bookmark icon fills and the server returns the story with saved:true), but the 'Saved' feed view shows 'No stories found.' — the saved story never appears in the Saved feed.

submitted_data.description: After clicking 'Save story' on a story card — which fills the bookmark icon and is confirmed by the server returning the story with "saved": true — switching the feed selector to the 'Saved' view shows 'No stories found.'. The just-saved story never appears in the Saved feed, so the save-and-view-saved journey cannot be completed.

evidence_result.assessment: Screenshot 1 shows the Digg feed with the first story's bookmark icon appearing filled/solid after clicking save. Screenshot 2 shows the feed selector changed to 'Saved' with 'No stories found.' displayed. Screenshot 3 confirms the same empty saved feed state. The evidence strongly supports the root cause: the PATCH save endpoint returns the story with saved:true (200), but the subsequent GET /api/feed?feed=saved returns an empty stories array (200), a clear server-side contradiction. All factual claims (saved:true in response, empty stories array, story-list.tsx rendering, story-card.tsx line references) are verified in the evidence results. The root cause is stated definitively as a server-side defect evidenced by contradictory API responses, without speculating about internal implementation. Interaction evidence requirements are met for both interaction steps.

requirements_result.assessment: The bug report documents a clear, reproducible functional failure: saving a story succeeds (PATCH returns saved:true, bookmark icon fills), but switching to the Saved feed shows 'No stories found.' because GET /api/feed?feed=saved returns an empty stories array. The screenshots clearly show: [Image 1] the feed with the bookmark icon on the story card, [Image 2] the feed selector changed to 'Saved' showing the empty state, and [Image 3] confirming the persistent empty state. The root cause correctly identifies this as a server-side contradiction — the save endpoint confirms the story is saved but the saved feed endpoint returns no stories. This follows the guideline exception for server responses that contradict observable application state, so no backend recording analysis is required. The causal chain is well-traced with network evidence showing both the PATCH response (saved:true) and the GET response (empty stories array). Cross-run interference is not a plausible explanation since the save was performed by this journey and the server's own feed endpoint fails to return it. This is a clear core functionality failure that prevents the save-and-view-saved workflow.
